lyrics:
We said, "This is one of those days"
You held my hand like you knew
We weren't just walking through the now-
We were five years ahead somehow
Looking back at morning dew
The path, the sky, our footsteps too

We weren’t just here
We were then, assembled
Bit by bit, a gentle tremble
In the shape of what we’d miss…

Memory assembly-
Layered light through falling leaves
Moments packed like whispered dreams
We’ll unwrap them when we need
Memory assembly-
We built the future out of now
We said we’d hold this somehow
And here we are
Holding it down

There’s laughter caught in the corners of dusk
And echoes in old oak bark
The weight of the words we didn’t rush-
Like “this will mean something someday”
And we meant it like a spark
Thrown forward into the dark

Time's not a line-it loops and bends
And some moments become old friends
Waiting in reverie

Memory assembly-
Laid in layers, heart and bone
Threads we gathered on our own
To stitch into our unknown
Memory assembly-
Promises with open hands
Moments we could barely stand
To let go
But we did-
And here they land

"Will we remember this?" you said
"We already do," I smiled instead
The future's built of present bliss
And I'll keep building it like this

Memory assembly-
Not just shadows on the wall
But the love that holds us all
When we said we'd catch the fall
Memory assembly-
The now inside the then
The moment we begin
To remember
Again

We said we’d remember
And we are
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This indie rock track has a nostalgic and hopeful atmosphere, built on a texture of clean, arpeggiated electric guitars and earnest male vocals. The energy builds dynamically from intimate, reflective verses into a driving and expansive chorus, creating a feel that is both thoughtful and anthemic. The production is polished and spacious, with each element sitting clearly in the mix, and the chorus swells into a dense, wide arrangement where layered vocals and guitars blend seamlessly. A distinctive quality is the powerful transition into the soaring "Memory assembly" chorus, which serves as the emotional and energetic peak of the song.

review.review_text: PCBender has always had a feel for the big emotional swing, and 'Memory Assembly' is him swinging for the seats. The lyric 'Will we remember this? / We already do' lands with the kind of quiet certainty that good songwriting earns rather than announces, and the chorus delivery earns it: a clean, arpeggiated intro that opens into a wide, layered swell where the vocals and guitars stack into something genuinely anthemic. At a brisk 143 BPM in E minor, it never feels hurried, just purposeful, the way a Springsteen mid-tempo builds before it crests. A highlight, and one of his most accessible entries.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'Will we remember this? / We already do'
arpeggiated intro building to wide choral swell
